<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8" standalone="no" ?>
<SnowReport>
	<_LastUpdated>2026-07-19T13:54:48</_LastUpdated>
	<TwentyFourHourSnowfall>0</TwentyFourHourSnowfall>
	<CurrentSnowdepth>41</CurrentSnowdepth>
	<AvSnowdepthinSnowmakingAreas>107</AvSnowdepthinSnowmakingAreas>
	<AvSnowdepthLastMeasuredOn>19/07/2026</AvSnowdepthLastMeasuredOn>
	<DateofLastSnowfall>14/07/2026</DateofLastSnowfall>
	<SevenDaySnowfall>9</SevenDaySnowfall>
	<SeasonToDateSnowfall>96</SeasonToDateSnowfall>
	<Snowmaking>On Standby</Snowmaking>
	<WeatherConditionsasat6AM></WeatherConditionsasat6AM>
	<Temperature>7.4</Temperature>
	<OvernightMin>5.4</OvernightMin>
	<ForecastMax>9</ForecastMax>
	<Wind>Calm 0-10 km/h</Wind>
	<WindDirection>WSW</WindDirection>
	<Precipitation>None</Precipitation>
	<ExpectedVisibility>Excellent</ExpectedVisibility>
	<ExpectedCloudCover>Sunny</ExpectedCloudCover>
	<Forecast>Sunny. Light winds becoming northeasterly 15 to 20 km/h in the evening then tending northerly 20 to 25 km/h in the late evening.</Forecast>
	<ResortReportHeadline>Glorious Repeat!</ResortReportHeadline>
	<ResortReport><p>It'll be yet another gloriously sunny and mostly calm day on the mountain.&nbsp; The sky is clear, the current temp is a balmy 7.4 degrees, there is no wind, and we are heading for another very warm top temp of 9 degrees.&nbsp; Although it's been warm again overnight, the snow has had another low humidity freeze and will be firm first thing before the sunny aspects start to soften for fun spring-like conditions.&nbsp; Shaded aspects will stay hard and slick all day.&nbsp;&nbsp;</p><p>The Summit will open at 7.30am this morning, followed by all other scheduled lifts at 9am. The Big D will remain open until 6pm. It's the last day of extended hours before we revert back to normal operational hours, so be sure to make the most of it! From tomorrow, Summit and the Big D will spin from 8.30am-5pm.&nbsp;</p><p>Heavenly Valley Lift is closed and the area is not recommended. The cover is marginal and the surface will remain icy all day on the shaded aspects.&nbsp; The conditions are only suitable for advanced and expert skiers and riders, so think about your group's ability before traversing across from the Summit or Road Runner.&nbsp; The Extreme Area is closed due to an extreme ice hazard.&nbsp; Do not enter!</p><p>Slap on the screen and have another beaut day on the hill.</p><p>Cheers Bill</p><p><br></p></ResortReport>
	<HideReportEDMWeb>False</HideReportEDMWeb>
	<LiftsOpen></LiftsOpen>
	<LiftsStandby></LiftsStandby>
	<LiftsClosed></LiftsClosed>
	<RunsOpen></RunsOpen>
	<RunsGroomed></RunsGroomed>
	<RunsSnowmaking>5</RunsSnowmaking>
	<TerrainParks></TerrainParks>
	<Backcountry>19 July 2026
The cover is marginal and there is a very significant sliding hazard in the backcountry.   The sunniest of aspects may soften for spring-like turns as the day warms but shaded and south facing slopes will likely stay very icy and dangerous for the day.  Take Care. </Backcountry>
	<Avalanche></Avalanche>
	<HeliLink>Closed</HeliLink>
</SnowReport>
